name: My lungs are messed up from years at the factory in Salt Lake City, is this an occupational illness I can claim workers comp for?
description: Yes, lung issues from factory exposure in Utah qualify under the ODA if linked to work; file promptly with medical proof.[1][9]

mainEntity.name: My lungs are messed up from years at the factory in Salt Lake City, is this an occupational illness I can claim workers comp for?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, lung issues from factory exposure in Utah qualify under the ODA if linked to work; file promptly with medical proof.[1][9]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, if your lung condition like lead poisoning or other diseases directly resulted from workplace exposure, it qualifies as an occupational illness under Utah's Occupational Disease Act (ODA).[1][9] Utah separates occupational diseases from regular injuries, treating them under the ODA which provides exclusive remedies distinct from the Workers' Compensation Act (WCA).[1] Key factors include proving the illness arose from work conditions, such as handling scrap lead, and not a pre-existing condition unrelated to employment.[1][3] Report to your employer immediately, gather medical records linking exposure to symptoms, and file with the Utah Labor Commission within deadlines; consult a specialist if denied.[6] Acting quickly preserves your benefits like medical care and wage replacement.
